editor_note: The prose is otherwise genuinely fluent, well-restructured German with correct and consistent quantum/linear-algebra terminology (Dichtematrizen, positiv semidefinit, Spektralsatz, Bilinearität, Skalarprodukt, Kraus-/Choi-/Stinespring-Darstellung) and a consistent du/wir register — it reads like a German mathematician wrote it. However, one genuine semantic drift in the proof ("orthonormal set" rendered as "Orthonormalbasis"/orthonormal basis) is mathematically misleading and is decisive for a FAIL; a leftover untranslated English word ("straightforward") and an awkward superlative ("linksten/rechtsten") are additional, lesser slips.

examples.source: we conclude that the $n$ column vectors ... form an orthonormal set
examples.translation: schließen wir, dass die $n$ Spaltenvektoren ... eine Orthonormalbasis bilden
examples.suggested: ... ein Orthonormalsystem / eine orthonormale Menge bilden
examples.why: "set" was strengthened to "Basis". These n vectors are only the first n of the mN columns of the unitary U (the space has dimension mN > n in general); the very next sentence extends them via Gram-Schmidt to complete the matrix. Calling them an orthonormal *basis* is mathematically wrong here and undercuts the pedagogical point of the extension step.

examples.translation: ミッドサーキット測定や古典演算に時間が必要であるにもかかわらず
examples.suggested: 回路途中の測定や古典演算に時間が必要であるにもかかわらず
examples.why: "mid-circuit measurement" is translated 回路途中の測定 everywhere else (L18, L254, L799, L966) but becomes the katakana ミッドサーキット測定 here in the Step 4 prose — a third inconsistent core term.

editor_note: placeholder

examples.source: "unitary": A dense unitary matrix simulation of an ideal circuit. This simulates the unitary matrix of the circuit itself
examples.translation: "unitary": 이상적인 Circuit의 밀집 단일 행렬 시뮬레이션입니다. … Circuit 자체의 단일 행렬을 시뮬레이션합니다.
examples.suggested: 이상적인 Circuit의 밀집 유니터리 행렬 시뮬레이션입니다. … Circuit 자체의 유니터리 행렬을 시뮬레이션합니다.
examples.why: "unitary" here is the quantum-computing term 'unitary matrix' (유니터리 행렬), not '단일' (= single/sole). '단일 행렬' misreads as 'single matrix' and loses the meaning that this method simulates the circuit's unitary operator; the error repeats in the next sentence.

editor_note: Reads like fluent, well-edited Korean technical prose throughout — natural teaching register in consistent 해요체/-ㅂ니다, no translationese or calqued syntax, and core domain terms (Qubit, Circuit, Backend, 충실도, 트랜스파일, 트리/체인/노드/에지, 커플링 맵, 오류 억제) are conventional and used consistently. The one real slip is the gloss for "betweenness centrality" rendered as 매개변수 중심성 ("parameter centrality"); the field term is 매개 중심성 / 사이 중심성, so the Korean word is wrong even though the English is kept in parentheses. A second, minor inconsistency: "device" is rendered as 장치 early on but 디바이스 later in the file.

examples.source: The node with the highest value of betweenness centrality is at the center of the graph.
examples.translation: 매개변수 중심성(betweenness centrality) 값이 가장 높은 노드가 그래프의 중심에 위치합니다.
examples.suggested: 매개 중심성(betweenness centrality) 값이 가장 높은 노드가 그래프의 중심에 위치합니다.
examples.why: 매개변수 means 'parameter'; the graph-theory term betweenness centrality is conventionally 매개 중심성 or 사이 중심성. A reader could misread it as 'parameter centrality.' The English parenthetical mitigates but the Korean word itself is wrong.

examples.source: ...on a Heron device. (vs earlier 'an Eagle device')
examples.translation: Heron 디바이스 / Eagle 디바이스 (line 1052/1057) vs Eagle 장치 (line 24/173/178)
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering for 'device' (디바이스 or 장치) and use it throughout.
examples.why: Within-file inconsistency for the same term 'device' — 장치 in the early sections, 디바이스 in the Heron section. Not misleading, purely a consistency slip.

editor_note: Reads like native, well-edited Korean technical prose — idiomatic connectives, consistent 합니다체 instructional register, faithful meaning, and it teaches the intuition (waves/Fourier, basis change, commutators) as clearly as the English. The one real blemish is within-file terminology inconsistency for "uncertainty": headings/relations use 불확정성/불확정도 (the conventional physics term, as in 불확정성 원리) while the "Calculating uncertainty" section body switches to 불확실성, mixing two renderings of the same concept. This does not mislead the reader, hence MINOR not FAIL; a single consistent choice (불확정성/불확정도) would be cleaner.

examples.source: We can quantify this using uncertainty, or variance. The "uncertainty" is often defined to be the square root of the "variance"
examples.translation: 이를 불확실성(uncertainty) 또는 분산(variance)을 사용하여 정량화할 수 있습니다. "불확실성"은 흔히 분포의 "분산"의 제곱근으로 정의됩니다.
examples.suggested: 불확정성(uncertainty) ... "불확정성"은 ...
examples.why: This section renders "uncertainty" as 불확실성, but the rest of the file (title "불확정성 탐구하기", "불확정성 관계", and the quantity 불확정도) uses 불확정성/불확정도 — the conventional Korean physics term (불확정성 원리). 불확실성 is the generic word and is off-convention for this physics concept; the within-file switch is inconsistent.

examples.source: the uncertainty for some observable S is denoted ΔS (the Δ quantity, used throughout)
examples.translation: 관측량 $S$에 대한 불확실성은 $\Delta S$로 표기하며 ... (이후 불확정도의 곱)
examples.suggested: choose one rendering of the Δ quantity throughout (불확정도)
examples.why: The Δ quantity is 불확실성 in the Calculating-uncertainty intro (L300) but 불확정도 elsewhere (e.g. L601 "X에 대한 불확정도", L609 "불확정도의 곱"). Same quantity, two terms; 불확정도 is the better consistent choice.

editor_note: Sentence-level prose is genuinely fluent and natural — it reads like a Korean quantum engineer wrote it, the 합니다체 written register is consistent (no honorific/humble slips), and I found no semantic drift: meanings, conditions, and quantities all track the English faithfully. The reason this is a FAIL is pervasive within-file terminology inconsistency from an unreconciled two-pass merge: the same core terms are rendered multiple ways — observable (관측 가능량 / 관측값 / 관측량 / left in English), bounds (경계 / 바운드), lightcone (라이트콘 / 광원뿔), noise (노이즈 / 잡음), post-selection (후처리·사후 선택·후선택·English), and circuit/qubit/gate/backend left in English in the Background+Step 0-2 sections but switched to 회로/큐비트/게이트/백엔드 from the Box-the-circuit section onward, sometimes in adjacent paragraphs. Recommend a single terminology pass: pick 관측량, 경계, 라이트콘, 노이즈, 후선택, and the Hangul forms 회로/큐비트/게이트/백엔드, and apply them file-wide.

examples.source: observable
examples.translation: 관측 가능량 (L14-18) vs 관측값 (L120,122) vs 관측량 (L629) vs "observable" left in English (L427,770,788)
examples.suggested: Pick one — e.g. 관측량 (or 관측 가능량) — and use it everywhere

examples.source: bounds

examples.why: The forward/backward 'bounds' the whole SLC workflow is built around flip between 경계 and the transliteration 바운드 depending on which translation pass wrote the section.

examples.source: circuit / qubit / gate / backend
examples.translation: kept in English in L14-339 & L202, but 회로/큐비트/게이트/백엔드 from ~L335 onward (L335,339,425,539,1009)
examples.suggested: Choose one policy per term and apply file-wide (the later Hangul forms 회로/큐비트/게이트 are the conventional KO domain choices)

examples.source: noise / post-selection / noise rates
examples.translation: 노이즈 (file) vs 잡음 (L1009); 후처리 vs 사후 선택 (L202) vs 후선택 (L41,1009) vs English (L905); 노이즈 율 (L14,32) vs 노이즈 비율 (L629,631)
examples.suggested: Standardize on 노이즈, 후선택, and 노이즈 율 throughout

examples.source: lightcone
examples.translation: 라이트콘 (L16,18,22) vs 광원뿔 (L513)
examples.suggested: Use 라이트콘 (matches section titles and SLC = '음영 라이트콘')
examples.why: The headline concept of the addon ('shaded lightcone') is suddenly calqued as 광원뿔 in one visualization paragraph, breaking the link to the established 라이트콘 term.

examples.source: (positive control) overall prose quality
examples.translation: e.g. L457 순방향 경계와 역방향 전파 경계 중 더 작은 값을 취합니다
examples.suggested: (no change needed)
examples.why: For the record: sentence-level fluency and accuracy are strong and native-sounding; the FAIL is purely cross-section term consistency, not MT-style prose.

editor_note: Fluent, well-edited 해요체 technical prose that reads as natively written and faithfully tracks the English meaning, including correctly handling a typo in the source. Core domain terminology is correct and consistent (큐비트, 관측량, 기댓값, 해밀토니안, 본드 차원, 게이트); the only real blemish is rendering "unitary" as 단일 연산 instead of 유니타리, plus a minor near-miss on "readout" (읽기 vs 판독).

examples.source: a Floquet unitary $\hat{U}_{\text{KI}}$ ... unitary evolution
examples.translation: Floquet 단일 연산 ... 단일 연산 ... 구현하는
examples.suggested: Floquet 유니타리(연산자) ... 유니타리 시간 진화
examples.why: "unitary" is the linear-algebra term (a unitary operator/evolution); 단일 연산 reads as "single operation" and loses the unitary-matrix meaning. The conventional Korean term is 유니타리(연산자). Consistent within file (lines 33, 59) but consistently off-target.

examples.source: characterizing the readout error ... readout mitigation
examples.translation: 읽기 오류를 특성화합니다 ... 읽기 완화
examples.suggested: 판독 오류 ... 판독 완화
examples.why: "readout" in QC is conventionally 판독/측정 판독; 읽기 ("read") is an understandable but non-standard near-miss. Used consistently, so not misleading.

examples.source: let's consider a single circuit and observable as our PUB
examples.translation: 단일 회로와 관측량을 PUB로 고려해 봅시다
examples.suggested: 단일 회로와 관측량을 PUB로 삼아 봅시다 / 생각해 봅시다
examples.why: "PUB로 고려해 봅시다" is a slight calque of "consider ... as"; a native technical writer would more naturally say 삼겠습니다/생각해 봅시다. Grammatical and clear, purely a fluency nit.

editor_note: Faithful, natural Malay technical prose that reads as written by a domain-aware translator, not raw MT; meaning is fully preserved including the "out-of-the-box", "memory option", and "runs locally on the user's machine" caveats. Domain terms are correct and consistent (penyedia for provider, primitif, pengurangan ralat for error mitigation, antara muka, pilihan for option). Only isolated slips: "ukuran" is used for "measurement" where "pengukuran" is the standard term, and English-kept terms "Gate"/"Backend" are capitalized mid-sentence — both cosmetic and non-misleading.

examples.source: it offers no measurement or gate error mitigation implementations
examples.translation: ia tidak menawarkan implementasi pengurangan ralat ukuran atau Gate
examples.suggested: ia tidak menawarkan implementasi pengurangan ralat pengukuran atau get
examples.why: "ukuran" means size/measure; the conventional Malay term for "measurement" in this context is "pengukuran". Also "Gate" is needlessly capitalized mid-sentence (would normally be lowercase "get" or left as lowercase English).

examples.source: used with an arbitrary `backend` object
examples.translation: boleh digunakan dengan objek `backend` mana-mana
examples.suggested: boleh digunakan dengan mana-mana objek `backend`
examples.why: Postposing "mana-mana" after the noun is slightly stiff calqued word order; a native technical writer would front it as "mana-mana objek `backend`". Grammatical but not the most idiomatic phrasing.

editor_note: Native, well-edited Malay technical prose that teaches as clearly as the English; sentence structures are idiomatic rather than calqued, and the inclusive "kita" register matches the English "we'll" voice throughout. Core terminology is correct conventional Malay (surih = trace, semipositif pasti = positive semidefinite, nilai eigen, keadaan tulen = pure state, unjuran = projection, pangkat = rank) and is used consistently within the file. Only two minor slips: "Qubit" is needlessly capitalized (English uses lowercase), and a couple of slightly literal calques ("ada satu makna di mana", "darjah di mana") — none mislead or distort meaning.

examples.source: Indeed, there is a sense in which the choice to represent quantum states by density matrices leads naturally to the entire mathematical description of quantum information.
examples.translation: Sesungguhnya, ada satu makna di mana pilihan untuk mewakili keadaan kuantum dengan matriks ketumpatan membawa secara semula jadi kepada keseluruhan penerangan matematik maklumat kuantum.
examples.suggested: Sesungguhnya, dalam erti kata tertentu, pilihan untuk mewakili keadaan kuantum dengan matriks ketumpatan membawa secara semula jadi kepada keseluruhan penerangan matematik maklumat kuantum.
examples.why: "ada satu makna di mana" is a literal calque of "there is a sense in which"; "dalam erti kata tertentu" is the idiomatic Malay rendering. Meaning is preserved, so this is a polish point, not a defect.

editor_note: The prose reads fluently and natively within each section and meaning is faithful to the English throughout (I verified the technical claims — probabilities, expectation values, phase signs, Hadamard-as-rotation — all match), so this is not a mistranslation problem. The defect is pervasive within-file inconsistency: the document splits into a formal first half and a casual second half ("√NOT" section onward) that diverge on core domain terms — observable ("boleh cerapan" vs "boleh ukur"), coin ("duit syiling" vs "syiling"), qubit ("qubit" vs "Qubit"), gate ("gate" vs "Gate"), and constructive/destructive interference ("membina/memusnah" vs "konstruktif/destruktif") — plus a register break (contractions "dah/nak/tapi/kalau" appear only in the second half). With ≥3 inconsistent core terms this crosses the FAIL bar; it's the classic signature of a sync-refresh that re-rendered half the file in a different style.

examples.source: the expectation value of an observable / observables X, Y, and Z
examples.translation: L195/203 "nilai jangkaan bagi sebuah boleh cerapan" vs L554 "nilai jangkaan tiga boleh ukur"
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering of "observable" (e.g. "boleh cerap"/"kuantiti boleh cerap") and use it everywhere.
examples.why: The same core term "observable" is rendered two different ways in the same file, confusing a learner who is meeting the concept for the first time.

examples.suggested: Standardize on one term for the coin throughout (the body's "duit syiling kuantum" / "syiling kuantum").
examples.why: The coin is the recurring analogy that the whole module is built on; switching the noun midway is jarring and reads like two translators.

examples.source: qubit / gate (lowercase technical terms)

examples.suggested: Keep lowercase "qubit" and "gate" consistently, as in the first half and the English.
examples.why: Inconsistent capitalization of the two most frequent domain terms signals an unedited refresh and looks unprofessional in teaching material.

examples.source: (neutral instructional English throughout)

examples.suggested: Match the formal-but-friendly register of the first half ("sudah", "hendak/ingin", "Tetapi", "Jika").

examples.suggested: pekalinya adalah kompleks

editor_note: Reads as native, well-edited Malay technical prose in the common convention that retains core domain terms in English (Gate, Circuit, Qubit, Backend, Transpile, observable, swap, routing) while translating the explanatory scaffolding; meaning is faithful throughout and the key term "expectation value" is rendered consistently as the correct conventional "nilai jangkaan." Terminology is correct and consistent; only isolated minor slips exist: "arbitrarily chosen" softened to "dipilih secara rawak" (randomly), two different roots used for "decompose" (didekomposkan vs penguraian), and the counterfactual "would otherwise be introduced" slightly flattened — none of which mislead the reader.

examples.source: Here is an example of an arbitrarily chosen subexperiment generated from the QPD circuit.
examples.translation: Berikut adalah contoh subeksperimen yang dipilih secara rawak yang dijana daripada Circuit QPD.
examples.suggested: ...subeksperimen yang dipilih secara sewenang-wenangnya...
examples.why: "arbitrarily" (arbitrary pick, here index 100) is rendered as "secara rawak" (randomly). Mild semantic shift; harmless in context since the index is indeed an arbitrary choice.

examples.source: the decomposed gates' joint quasiprobability distribution / one for each gate decomposition
examples.translation: Gate yang didekomposkan ... setiap penguraian Gate
examples.suggested: use one root consistently, e.g. "penguraian"/"diuraikan" throughout
examples.why: Two different Malay roots used for decompose (loan "dekompos" vs native "urai") within the same file — minor within-file inconsistency, not misleading.

examples.source: avoiding the swap gates that would otherwise be introduced by routing
examples.translation: mengelakkan swap gates yang akan diperkenalkan semasa routing
examples.why: The counterfactual "would otherwise be introduced" is slightly flattened to a plain future "yang akan diperkenalkan"; the intended meaning (swaps routing would add) is still recoverable from context.

editor_note: Reads as fluent, well-edited technical Malay that teaches as clearly as the English; meaning is faithful throughout the prose paragraphs (background, options setup, job-tracking, plotting comparison, licensing note). Terminology is conventional and internally consistent: "qubit" and "job" kept in English, "tembakan" for shots, "litar kuantum" for quantum circuit, "jalinan" for entanglement, "orbital aktif/beku" and "penumpuan tenaga" for convergence — no domain-term errors or within-file inconsistencies. Informal "anda" register holds. Only one isolated naturalness slip ("Dalam jalankan fungsi utama") that does not mislead.

examples.source: In the main function run, you need to define the `max_states` and `max_expansion_states`

editor_note: Native, well-edited technical Polish throughout — this is a build/deployment guide with almost no quantum-domain terms, and the infrastructure vocabulary (niezależna od dostawcy, warstwa pośrednicząca, kompilacja skrośna, środowisko uruchomieniowe) is rendered with correct conventional terms, while dev jargon (stub, wheel, host, API) is consistently kept in English with proper Polish declension. Informal "ty" register holds consistently with no formal slips, and meaning is faithful; only two isolated, non-misleading slips keep it from flawless.

examples.source: If you specify `trace`, you can find underlying HTTP transaction logs.

examples.suggested: ...zobaczysz logi niskopoziomowych transakcji HTTP.

editor_note: Semantic fidelity, pedagogy, and informal "ty" register are all solid — meaning is faithful throughout and most prose reads naturally, like a Polish technical writer wrote it. The verdict is driven by a real domain-terminology defect: in the "State of the technology" bullets, "gate" and "circuit" are left as capitalized English proper nouns with Polish apostrophe-inflection ("Gate'ów", "Gate'y", "Circuitów") instead of the standard, conventional Polish terms bramka/obwód — terms the same file uses correctly elsewhere ("obwodów kwantowych", L82). Compounding this, "quantum computing" and "quantum advantage" are rendered inconsistently across the file (sometimes translated to "obliczenia/informatyka kwantowa" / "przewaga kwantowa", sometimes left in English), which a native editor would normalize.

examples.suggested: stanowi wyzwanie dla obwodów z qubitami nadprzewodzącymi.

examples.source: quantum advantage ... a quantum advantage

examples.suggested: Choose one rendering and use it consistently (either keep "quantum advantage" as a branded term everywhere, or use "przewaga kwantowa" everywhere).

examples.source: quantum computing
examples.translation: informatyka kwantowa (L28/35/37) / obliczenia kwantowe (L86) / quantum computing left in EN (L152/182/190/227/234/235)
examples.suggested: Normalize to a single rendering of "quantum computing" across the file.

examples.source: It's results can still be matched by advanced techniques running on classical computers

editor_note: The translated prose is, where it is actually translated, fluent and native-sounding (the Background, Options, "Wow", and final entanglement sections read like a Polish physicist wrote them, and the informal "ty" register holds consistently). But the file fails on terminology: from the "Test using real quantum computers" section onward, three core domain terms are left untranslated as capitalized English hybrids — "Circuit"/"Circuits", "Gate"/"Gate'ów", "Qubit"/"jednoQubitowy" — even though the SAME concepts are rendered with the correct, standard Polish terms elsewhere in the very same file (obwód, bramka, kubit/qubit). This is a within-file inconsistency on every key term plus a character-name flip (Lucas → Łukasz only in Step 4), so a student is taught with broken, non-idiomatic vocabulary in exactly the hands-on coding half of the lesson.

examples.source: We want to make a quantum circuit ... we'll need to review some basic gates
examples.translation: Chcemy zbudować Circuit kwantowy ... musimy przypomnieć sobie podstawowe Gate'y w obliczeniach kwantowych
examples.suggested: Chcemy zbudować obwód kwantowy ... musimy przypomnieć sobie podstawowe bramki
examples.why: "Circuit" and "Gate" left in English with Polish inflection; the standard, universal Polish terms are "obwód" and "bramka" — and both are used correctly elsewhere in this same file (e.g. "obwody kwantowe" L841, "bramki Hadamarda" L912).

examples.source: qubits start out initialized in the |0> state ... the qubit's projection along z
examples.translation: Qubity są inicjalizowane w stanie |0> ... rzut Qubitu wzdłuż z
examples.why: "Qubit" rendered as a mid-word-capitalized English hybrid throughout the gates/circuit section (even "jednoQubitowy", "dwu-Qubitową"), reading like a leaked glossary-protected token; standard Polish is "kubit" or lowercase "qubit".

examples.source: Lucas and Rihanna measure one sign of the spin projection
examples.translation: Łukasz i Rihanna mierzą jeden ... znak rzutu spinu
examples.suggested: Lucas i Rihanna ...
examples.why: The character "Lucas" is kept as "Lucas" throughout the file but is abruptly Polonized to "Łukasz" only in the Step 4 recap (4 occurrences, L841), so the same observer has two names within one lesson.

examples.source: a Qiskit primitive called the StatevectorSampler ... sample all the possible states
examples.translation: prymitywu Qiskit o nazwie StatevectorSampler ... próbkowania wszystkich możliwych stanów
examples.why: This one is handled WELL ("prymityw", "próbkowanie", "shotów") — cited as the positive contrast showing the translator knows the conventions; it makes the Circuit/Gate/Qubit lapses in the surrounding paragraphs clearly an inconsistency rather than a deliberate convention.

editor_note: Core domain nouns are left untranslated and capitalized mid-sentence throughout the prose (Circuit, Qubit, Gate, Backend) — e.g. "siedmioQubitowego Circuit", "dwa Circuit po cztery Qubit każdy", "przewodów Qubit" — which no Polish quantum engineer would write; the standard Polish "kubit / obwód / bramka" conventions are abandoned, so the prose reads as raw glossary-protected MT rather than native technical writing. "Gate" is rendered inconsistently within the file (translated as "bramka SWAP" on line 78 but left as English "Gate" on lines 117/171), and "kwaziprawa" (line 171) is an outright mistranslation of "quasiprobability" (correct: "kwaziprawdopodobieństwo"). The informal "ty" register holds correctly and "obserwowalna" is a consistent, defensible rendering, but the pervasive English-noun injection plus the term mistranslation push this to FAIL.

examples.source: reconstruct expectation values of a seven-qubit circuit ... splitting it into two four-qubit circuits
examples.translation: zrekonstruujemy wartości oczekiwane siedmioQubitowego Circuit, dzieląc go na dwa czteroQubitowe Circuit
examples.suggested: zrekonstruujemy wartości oczekiwane siedmiokubitowego obwodu, dzieląc go na dwa czterokubitowe obwody
examples.why: Core nouns (qubit, circuit) left in English and capitalized mid-word/mid-sentence; glossary-protection artifact, not Polish a native engineer would write.

examples.source: non-local gates spanning more than one partition will be cut
examples.translation: nielokalne Gate obejmujące więcej niż jedną partycję zostaną przecięte
examples.suggested: nielokalne bramki obejmujące więcej niż jedną partycję zostaną przecięte
examples.why: "Gate" is left in English here but translated as "bramka" on line 78 ("bramka SWAP") — a within-file inconsistency for a core term.

examples.source: the decomposed gates' joint quasiprobability distribution
examples.translation: rozkładu kwaziprawa łączonego dekompozowanych Gate
examples.suggested: łącznego rozkładu kwaziprawdopodobieństwa zdekomponowanych bramek
examples.why: "kwaziprawa" mistranslates "quasiprobability" (reads as "quasi-law"); correct term is "kwaziprawdopodobieństwo". Phrase is also near-incomprehensible.

examples.source: the reset operation will cause the state of the remainder of the system to be partially collapsed
examples.translation: operacja reset spowoduje częściowe zapaść stanu reszty systemu
examples.suggested: operacja reset spowoduje częściowe zapadnięcie stanu reszty systemu
examples.why: Grammatical-gender mismatch ("częściowe zapaść") yields broken Polish; meaning survives but the noun phrase is ungrammatical.

examples.source: with ... the `Move` operations in place
examples.translation: z ... operacjami `Move` na swoich miejscach
examples.suggested: z ... rozmieszczonymi operacjami `Move`
examples.why: "na swoich miejscach" is a calque of "in place"; a native writer would say "w odpowiednich miejscach" or use "rozmieszczonymi".

examples.source: Here we are using a fake backend
examples.translation: Tutaj używamy fałszywego Backend
examples.suggested: Tutaj używamy atrapy backendu (fake backend)
examples.why: "Backend" left undeclined and capitalized; the uninflected English noun reads as MT — it should be lowercased and declined.

editor_note: Fluent, natural Polish that reads like a working quantum engineer wrote it; meaning is faithful throughout (all quantities, conditions, and the Pauli-string encoding examples match the English, no semantic drift). Core terminology is correct and consistent: "qubit", "wartość oczekiwana", "funkcja straty/celu", "graf/wierzchołki/krawędzie", and English-kept "ansatz"/"Hamiltonian"/"solver" are all used conventionally. The only blemishes are isolated naturalness/register slips — English-style Title Case on common-noun phrases ("Kodowanie Korelacji Pauliego") and the slightly stiff, capitalized "Wariacyjny Eigensolver Kwantowy (VQE)" — none of which mislead; informal "ty" register holds consistently.

examples.source: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE)
examples.translation: Wariacyjny Eigensolver Kwantowy (VQE)
examples.suggested: wariacyjny kwantowy eigensolver (VQE) — or keep the English name
examples.why: Word-by-word Title Case is an English capitalization calque, and the reordering with English-rooted "Eigensolver" reads stiffly; lowercase or keeping the established English name is more natural.

examples.source: Pauli Correlation Encoding (PCE)
examples.translation: Kodowaniem Korelacji Pauliego (PCE)
examples.suggested: kodowaniem korelacji Pauliego (PCE)
examples.why: Title-casing each word of a common-noun phrase mirrors English orthography; Polish capitalizes only the first word plus the proper name (Pauli). Harmless but a visible calque.

examples.source: due to its expressive capabilities
examples.translation: ze względu na jego możliwości ekspresyjne
examples.suggested: ze względu na jego ekspresywność / dużą zdolność wyrażania
examples.why: "możliwości ekspresyjne" is understandable but slightly clunky; a native technical writer would more likely use "ekspresywność" or "zdolność wyrażania". Minor, does not mislead.

editor_note: Reads as native, well-edited Brazilian Portuguese technical prose — idiomatic imperatives ("Acesse o diretório", "Navegue até"), correct subjunctive ("quando houver alterações"), and faithful rendering of the tricky multi-clause Rust admonition with its conditional intact. Domain/dev terminology is conventional and consistent (modo de depuração, desempenho em tempo de execução, ambiente virtual; "linting" correctly left in English), informal "você/seu" register holds throughout, and there is no semantic drift. Only stylistic note: "modo de lançamento" for "release mode" is a slightly literal choice (many PT devs prefer "modo release"/"modo de produção") but it is defensible, clear, and used consistently — not a defect.

examples.source: build the binary in _release mode_
examples.translation: recompilar o binário em _modo de lançamento_
examples.suggested: modo release / modo de produção (optional)
examples.why: Defensible literal rendering, applied consistently in both the body and the admonition; understandable to PT developers though 'modo release' is more common in the field. Stylistic only, not misleading.

editor_note: Native, well-edited technical Portuguese throughout — idiomatic choices like "nada mais é do que", "a saber", "graças ao fato de que" read like a Portuguese-speaking quantum engineer wrote them, not MT. Domain terminology is correct and consistent (estabilizador/geradores estabilizadores, autovetor/autovalor, observáveis, síndrome, verificação de paridade, comutam/anti-comutam); all eigenvalue signs, commute/anti-commute logic, and parity directions are faithful to the source. Sole blemish is the capitalized "um Gate $X$" (line ~153) for the English "X gate", which is stylistically inconsistent with the lowercase operation references elsewhere — a cosmetic slip, not a meaning error.

examples.source: the effect of this operation on an encoding is equivalent to an $X$ gate on the logical qubit
examples.translation: o efeito dessa operação sobre uma codificação é equivalente a um Gate $X$ no qubit lógico
examples.suggested: ...equivalente a uma porta $X$ (ou "a um gate $X$", minúsculo) no qubit lógico
examples.why: Capitalized "Gate" is an out-of-place term-base artifact; inconsistent with the lowercase "operações $Z$/operação $X$" used throughout the file. Cosmetic only — does not change meaning.

editor_note: The prose is genuinely native, fluent Brazilian Portuguese — idioms like "joia da coroa", "matrioska russa", "dar uma olhada por baixo do capô", and "ponto intermediário" read as a real quantum educator wrote them, the informal "você" register holds throughout, and meaning is faithful with no semantic drift. The one editorial blemish is a within-file terminology seam: the first half abbreviates quantum Fourier transform as TFQ while the second half (from "Desmembrando o algoritmo da QFT" onward) silently switches to the English QFT; the discrete transform is likewise TDF in the body but DFT in the True/False section. These are inconsistencies a reader would notice but they do not mislead.

examples.source: the quantum Fourier transform (QFT)
examples.suggested: Pick one abbreviation for quantum Fourier transform and use it throughout (TFQ or QFT), reconciling the mid-document switch.
examples.why: The same concept is abbreviated two different ways depending on the section, creating a visible inconsistency seam where the two translation passes meet.

examples.source: discrete Fourier transform (DFT)
examples.translation: L73 "transformada de Fourier (TDF)" vs L645 "transformada discreta de Fourier (DFT)"
examples.suggested: Use one abbreviation (TDF or DFT) consistently for the discrete transform.
examples.why: Minor echo of the same inconsistency: the body localizes the abbreviation to TDF but the True/False question reverts to the English DFT.

editor_note: Reads as native, well-edited Brazilian-Portuguese technical prose — fluent and faithful to the English with no semantic drift. Domain terminology is correct, conventional, and consistent: "portas" (gates), "valor esperado" (expectation value), "observável", "transpilar", "primitiva", and qubit/backend/sampling overhead/circuit cutting sensibly kept in English throughout. Informal register holds via the standard você-imperative headings and first-person-plural narration; the lowercase "hamiltoniano" faithfully mirrors the source's casing rather than being an error.

Reads like native technical Portuguese: fluent, faithful, and pedagogically equivalent to the English. Domain terminology is correct and internally consistent — portas (gates), emaranhamento (entanglement), estabilizador, primitivas Qiskit, observáveis, transpilação — with qubit/bit-flip/QEC sensibly kept in English per field convention. Informal "você" register holds throughout; only two trivially literal touches ("se concentram criticamente", "ofertas de conteúdo") that are fully clear and non-misleading, hence PASS.

examples.source: Stabilizer codes critically focus on correcting a discrete error set
examples.translation: Os códigos estabilizadores se concentram criticamente na correção de um conjunto de erros discretos
examples.suggested: Os códigos estabilizadores concentram-se essencialmente na correção de um conjunto discreto de erros
examples.why: Minor literal calque of 'critically focus'; a native writer might prefer 'essencialmente/fundamentalmente', but it is clear and not misleading (non-blocking).

examples.source: help us improve our content offerings and user experience
examples.translation: melhorar nossas ofertas de conteúdo e experiência do usuário
examples.suggested: melhorar nossa oferta de conteúdo e a experiência do usuário
examples.why: 'ofertas de conteúdo' is a slightly literal rendering of 'content offerings' but remains perfectly understandable (non-blocking).

editor_note: Faithful, fluent technical Romanian with correct and consistent core terminology (primitive, observabili, transpilare, suprimare/atenuare a erorilor, joburi, shot-uri); no semantic drift and it teaches as clearly as the English. The only blemishes are one informal-register slip ("Rețineți că" instead of the tu-form "Reține că", which the rest of the file maintains) and two minor naturalness/terminology slips ("atenuarea de citire" for "readout mitigation" reads incomplete, and "pasajele de transpilare" calques "transpiler passes" where "treceri/pase de transpilare" would be more idiomatic).

examples.source: Note that Open Plan users cannot submit session jobs.
examples.translation: Rețineți că utilizatorii planului Open nu pot trimite joburi de sesiune.
examples.suggested: Reține că utilizatorii planului Open nu pot trimite joburi de sesiune.
examples.why: Isolated formal-imperative slip; the rest of the file uses informal tu (îți permit, rulezi, vizitează). Should be the tu-form 'Reține'.

examples.source: readout mitigation
examples.translation: atenuarea de citire
examples.suggested: atenuarea erorilor de citire
examples.why: The English is 'readout mitigation' (mitigating readout errors); 'atenuarea de citire' drops the error referent and reads incomplete/awkward to a native technical reader.

examples.source: AI-powered transpiler passes
examples.translation: pasajele de transpilare alimentate de AI
examples.suggested: pasele/trecerile de transpilare bazate pe AI
examples.why: 'pasaje' (passages) is a literal calque of transpiler 'passes'; the conventional rendering is 'pase' or 'treceri'. Meaning still recoverable, so minor.

editor_note: Meaning is faithfully preserved throughout and the register is correctly informal, but the file fails the "three or more within-file term inconsistencies" criterion. The math and pedagogical prose are otherwise clear and accurate; this is a normalization/polish failure, not a comprehension one.

examples.source: a query gate
examples.translation: un gate de interogare (L192) vs poartă de interogare (L445)
examples.suggested: poartă de interogare
examples.why: Same 'query gate' concept rendered with English loanword 'gate' in the Qiskit sections and correct Romanian 'poartă' in the analysis sections — inconsistent within one file; 'poartă (cuantică)' is the conventional RO term.

examples.source: qubits
examples.translation: Qubiți (capitalized, L88-107) vs qubiți (L328,336)
examples.suggested: qubiți
examples.why: Mid-sentence capitalization of 'Qubit/Qubiți' is non-standard in Romanian and conflicts with the lowercase form used elsewhere in the same file.

examples.source: quantum circuit / Deutsch-Jozsa circuit
examples.translation: Circuit cuantic / Circuit-ul Deutsch-Jozsa (L192,235,323) vs circuitul Deutsch-Jozsa (L327,434)
examples.suggested: circuit cuantic / circuitul Deutsch-Jozsa
examples.why: 'Circuit' is capitalized mid-sentence in the implementation sections but lowercase in the analysis sections — same term, two casings.

examples.source: there's no randomness or uncertainty
examples.translation: nu există aleatoare sau incertitudine
examples.suggested: nu există aleatorietate sau incertitudine
examples.why: 'aleatoare' is the plural feminine adjective 'random', not the noun 'randomness'; reads as a translation slip a native writer would not produce.

examples.source: but through bad luck the queries all happen to return the same function value
examples.translation: dar din ghinioane interogările returnează toate aceeași valoare
examples.suggested: dar dintr-un ghinion, toate interogările returnează aceeași valoare
examples.why: Plural 'din ghinioane' is unidiomatic; the natural form is the singular 'din ghinion'. Meaning is preserved.

editor_note: Overall the prose is fluent, the informal-"tu" register holds, and most domain terminology is correct and consistent (qubiți, stare proprie, bază, măsurătoare, fidelitate, interceptare, pad de unică folosință, backend/sampler kept in English). However it carries a genuine and repeated mistranslation: "unbreakable encryption" is rendered "criptare de neîntrerupt" (= uninterrupted/continuous, not unbreakable) at lines 151 and 156 — a meaning error in the pedagogically central claim about one-time pads (correct would be "de nespart"/"imposibil de spart"). This is compounded by a within-file terminology inconsistency for "entangled" (anglicism "qubiți entangliați" at line 178 vs. the correct native "încâlciți/încâlcire" at line 296) and a real spelling defect ("Fotooni", "fotooniloruri" for fotoni/fotonilor, lines 177/180). Together these warrant FAIL; otherwise the translation reads natural and teaches well.

examples.source: a one-time pad is an unbreakable form of encryption
examples.translation: un pad de unică folosință este o formă de criptare de neîntrerupt
examples.suggested: ...o formă de criptare imposibil de spart (de nespart)
examples.why: "neîntrerupt" means uninterrupted/continuous, not unbreakable — it changes the core teaching claim. Recurs in the next paragraph ("Avem criptare de neîntrerupt...").

examples.source: Alice and Bob could share entangled qubits
examples.translation: Alice și Bob ar putea partaja qubiți entangliați
examples.suggested: ...qubiți încâlciți (entrelațați)
examples.why: "entangliați" is an anglicism inconsistent with the file's own correct native term: line 296 uses "qubiți încâlciți" / "încâlcire a qubiților". Same concept, two renderings within one file.

examples.source: Single photons can be detected ... for sharing photons
examples.translation: Fotooni individuali pot fi detectați ... pentru partajarea fotooniloruri
examples.suggested: Fotoni individuali ... pentru partajarea fotonilor
examples.why: "Fotooni" (double-o) and especially "fotooniloruri" are misspelled non-words; "fotooniloruri" also has a malformed double plural ending no native writer would produce.

examples.source: given a few assumptions, like:
examples.translation: dată câteva ipoteze, cum ar fi:
examples.suggested: presupunând câteva ipoteze / date fiind câteva ipoteze
examples.why: Calqued absolute-participle; "dată câteva ipoteze" fails noun agreement and reads ungrammatical to a native ear.

examples.source: understand that Eve making copies of the quantum state is forbidden by fundamental laws of nature
examples.translation: înțelege că Eve să facă copii ale stării cuantice este interzis de legile fundamentale ale naturii
examples.suggested: înțelege că faptul că Eve face copii ale stării cuantice este interzis...
examples.why: Calqued gerund-as-subject; the bare subjunctive "Eve să facă... este interzis" is stiff/non-idiomatic as a clause subject.

editor_note: Faithful, accurate translation with correct and consistent domain terminology (rețele tensoriale, stări produs matriceal/MPS, ansatz, fidelitate, eroare Trotter, magnetizare totală, cuplaj). Meaning is preserved throughout including the conditional and comparative claims. Two naturalness/register blemishes keep it from a clean PASS: common nouns are capitalized mid-sentence ("Circuit", "Backend", "Qubiți", "Transpiler") — a glossary-protection artifact that no native technical writer would produce — and the imperative register is inconsistent (informal "Consultă"/"folosești" at L176 vs. formal-plural "Rețineți" at L201). Neither misleads the reader.

examples.source: the resulting ISA circuit / two-qubit connectivity / fake backend
examples.translation: Circuit-ul ISA / conectivitatea cu doi Qubiți / Backend fals
examples.suggested: circuitul ISA / conectivitatea cu doi qubiți / backend fals
examples.why: Common nouns (circuit, qubit, backend, transpiler) are capitalized mid-sentence throughout the file. Romanian does not capitalize common nouns; this looks like a glossary-token artifact and reads unnaturally to a native eye, though it never changes meaning.

examples.source: See the API documentation ... / Note that, because we chose
examples.translation: Consultă documentația API ... (L176) / Rețineți că, deoarece am ales (L201)
examples.suggested: Use consistent informal singular: e.g. Reține că, deoarece am ales
examples.why: L176 uses informal singular imperative (Consultă) but L201 switches to the formal/plural imperative (Rețineți). The informal 'tu' convention is otherwise held; this is an isolated consistency slip.

examples.source: Determine how much of the time evolution to simulate classically
examples.translation: Determinarea cât din evoluția temporală urmează să fie simulată clasic
examples.why: Mildly stiff/calqued syntax; a native writer would phrase it as 'Determinarea cantității de evoluție temporală care urmează a fi simulată clasic' or 'Cât din evoluția temporală...'. Meaning is intact.

editor_note: Reads as native, well-edited Romanian technical prose with faithful meaning, correct pedagogy, and consistent informal "tu" register; core domain terminology is conventional and correct. Two real terminology slips keep it from a clean PASS: "shots" is mistranslated as "șocuri" (shocks/jolts) instead of conventional "shots"/"execuții", and "qubit" is rendered inconsistently — capitalized "Qubit/Qubiți" in prose but lowercase "qubiți" in the code-derived sentences. Neither inverts the physics, so it misleads only locally.

examples.source: executed using the matrix_product_state simulator with an arbitrary choice of 4096 shots
examples.translation: executată folosind simulatorul matrix_product_state cu un număr arbitrar de 4096 șocuri
examples.suggested: ...cu un număr arbitrar de 4096 de shots (execuții/repetări de măsurare)
examples.why: "șocuri" means "shocks/jolts" — a literal-but-wrong word. The quantum term "shots" (measurement repetitions) is kept in English or rendered "execuții/repetări" in Romanian; "șocuri" genuinely misleads a reader.

examples.source: qubit (throughout)
examples.translation: capitalized "Qubit/Qubiți" in prose (lines 20, 28, 39, 169, 208, 293, 295, 330) vs lowercase "qubiți" in code-derived text (lines 350, 393)
examples.suggested: use lowercase "qubit/qubiți" consistently
examples.why: Same core term rendered two ways within the file; also the mid-sentence capitalized "Qubit" is non-standard (convention is lowercase qubit) and reads as over-formalized.

examples.source: raw counts are normalized
examples.translation: Contorizările brute sunt normalizate
examples.suggested: Numărările brute sunt normalizate / Valorile brute (counts) sunt normalizate
examples.why: "Contorizare" (metering/tallying act) is an over-translation of "counts"; a native technical writer would use "numărări" or keep "counts". Minor, does not mislead.

editor_note: Native, well-edited Thai technical prose throughout. It uses the standard Thai code-switching convention for quantum writing—keeping core domain terms in English (qubit, circuit, observable, expectation value, Hamiltonian, transpile, noise, sampling, operator) inside a fluent Thai frame—and applies it consistently; conventionally-translated terms are correct and stable (ความลึก = circuit depth, ความเที่ยงตรงสูง = high-fidelity, ลดทอน = mitigate, การประมาณค่า = estimation). Meaning is faithful card-by-card, including tricky low-impact/negation and perfect-aspect phrasing, and register is consistently casual with no polite particles—reads as written by a Thai-speaking engineer, not as MT.

examples.source: These modular software components build on Qiskit's performant foundation and can plug into a workflow
examples.translation: modular software components เหล่านี้ต่อยอดจากรากฐานที่มีประสิทธิภาพของ Qiskit และเสียบเข้ากับ workflow
examples.why: ต่อยอดจากรากฐานที่มีประสิทธิภาพ for 'build on the performant foundation' is exactly how a Thai engineer would phrase it. เสียบเข้ากับ for 'plug into' is mildly literal but natural and clear — not a defect, noted only as the closest thing to a seam.

examples.source: removing noise model terms that have low impact on the observable estimation
examples.translation: นำ noise model terms ที่มีผลต่อการประมาณค่า observable น้อยออกไป
examples.why: Placement of น้อย (low) preserves the impact-level sense exactly — a spot MT often inverts. Confirms semantic fidelity rather than flagging a problem.

editor_note: Faithful, fluent technical Thai that teaches as clearly as the English: domain terms are correct and consistent (การประมาณเฟส = phase estimation, การหาอันดับ = order finding, เวกเตอร์เฉพาะ/ค่าเฉพาะ = eigenvector/eigenvalue, ยูนิทารี = unitary, มอดุโล = modulo), the stirring analogy and all probability/negation-heavy passages survive intact, and the informal register holds with no ครับ/ค่ะ. The only blemishes are isolated naturalness slips — rendering the verb "output" as ออก ("exit/leave"), one circular calque of "we take it to be implicit," and a slightly confusing "power-of-2" rendering — none of which mislead the math.

examples.source: Splitting even integers is easy: we just output 2 and N/2.
examples.translation: การแยกจำนวนคู่เป็นเรื่องง่าย: เราแค่ออก 2 และ N/2
examples.suggested: ...เราแค่ให้ผลลัพธ์เป็น 2 และ N/2
examples.why: "output" (verb) is rendered as ออก, which reads as "exit/go out," not "produce as output." Recurs in the algorithm steps (ให้ออก b... lines 589/595). Reader can infer intent, but a native technical writer would use ให้ผลลัพธ์เป็น / เอาต์พุต.

examples.source: ...which we haven't bothered to write — we take it to be implicit to avoid cluttering things up.
examples.translation: ...ซึ่งเราไม่ได้เขียนไว้ เรานัยว่ามันเป็นนัยโดยอ้อมเพื่อหลีกเลี่ยงความรกรุงรัง
examples.why: เรานัยว่ามันเป็นนัย is circular/redundant ("we imply that it is implied"), a stiff calque of "we take it to be implicit." Natural phrasing: เราถือว่าเป็นนัยโดยปริยาย.

examples.source: In fact, we only require *power-of-2* powers of a
examples.translation: ที่จริง เราต้องการเฉพาะกำลัง *กำลังสองของ 2* ของ a
examples.suggested: ...เราต้องการเฉพาะกำลังของ a ที่มีเลขชี้กำลังเป็นกำลังของ 2
examples.why: กำลังสองของ 2 reads literally as "the square of 2," obscuring that it means exponents that are powers of 2 (2,4,8,...). The following formula rescues the meaning, so it is minor.

editor_note: Reads as native, well-edited Thai technical prose written by someone fluent in the domain: physics terms (โมเมนตัมเชิงมุม "angular momentum", โมเมนต์แม่เหล็ก "magnetic moment", การฉายภาพ "projection") are conventional and used consistently, and computing terms the field keeps in English (Qubit, Gate, Circuit, transpile, eigenvalue, state vector, observable, Backend) are left in English consistently throughout — the idiomatic Thai-engineer choice. Meaning is faithful with no semantic drift, negation/quantity inversions, or dropped caveats; the casual register holds (uses คุณ, no ครับ/ค่ะ), and the pedagogical intuition and emphasis match the English. Only two trivial, non-misleading items: "bulky, classical magnets" softened to ขนาดใหญ่ ("large"), and a couple of common English-instructional calques like รู้สึกอิสระที่จะทดลอง for "feel free to play with" — both fully idiomatic in Thai teaching material.

examples.source: feel free to play with other values or even random values
examples.translation: รู้สึกอิสระที่จะทดลองค่าอื่นหรือแม้แต่ค่าสุ่ม
examples.suggested: ลองปรับเป็นค่าอื่นหรือค่าสุ่มได้ตามต้องการ
examples.why: Calque of "feel free to" (รู้สึกอิสระที่จะ); a native writer would phrase it more directly. Common and understandable, so not misleading.

examples.source: if they were exactly like bulky, classical magnets
examples.translation: หากมันเหมือนกับแม่เหล็กคลาสสิกขนาดใหญ่
examples.suggested: หากมันเหมือนกับแม่เหล็กคลาสสิกขนาดใหญ่ที่มีมวลมาก
examples.why: "bulky" (large + many-particle, which sets up the later heat-dissipation argument) flattened to just ขนาดใหญ่ "large"; the macroscopic-body nuance is mildly softened but the surrounding paragraph still makes the point.

editor_note: Reads as native, well-edited Thai technical prose.

examples.source: affecting the entangling gates in that box / map noise layers to the associated noise model
examples.translation: โมเดลสัญญาณรบกวนที่เรียนรู้มาแล้ว (L303); map noise layer กับโมเดลสัญญาณรบกวนที่เกี่ยวข้อง (L411)
examples.suggested: keep the English "noise model" as used elsewhere in the file (L14, L20, L203, L205), or convert all to สัญญาณรบกวน
examples.why: The same concept (noise / noise model) is rendered in English in most of the file but as Thai โมเดลสัญญาณรบกวน in three places, an internal inconsistency a careful technical editor would unify.

examples.source: This process is repeated for each anti-noise generator in the circuit.
examples.translation: กระบวนการนี้ทำซ้ำสำหรับแต่ละ anti-noise generator ในแต่ละ Circuit
examples.suggested: ...สำหรับแต่ละ anti-noise generator ใน Circuit
examples.why: EN refers to one circuit ('the circuit'); 'ในแต่ละ Circuit' reads as 'in each circuit', implying multiple circuits. Trivial number-agreement slip; does not change the procedure.

editor_note: Reads as well-edited, fluent Thai technical prose written by someone comfortable in the domain: it correctly keeps field-standard English terms untranslated (qubit, gate, ansatz, Hamiltonian, backend, coupling map, transpile, primitives, observable) and uses solid Thai renderings for translatable physics terms (ค่าคาดหวัง = expectation value, ฟิสิกส์สสารควบแน่น = condensed matter physics, ขอบเขตบน = upper bound, ทนทานต่อสัญญาณรบกวน = robust against noise). Meaning is faithful throughout — all caveats (non-strict linear chain, variational upper-bound guarantee, 2×maxiter call count, barren-plateau non-reproducibility) are preserved with no inversions or softening, and the casual register holds with no polite particles. The only blemishes are minor: "ตัวปรับแต่ง"/"การปรับแต่ง" for optimizer/optimization is a defensible, internally consistent convention but leans toward "tuner" rather than the more precise "ตัวหาค่าเหมาะที่สุด"; and "ผู้สมัครที่เหมาะสม" for "natural candidate" is a mild calque a native writer would phrase as "ตัวเลือกที่เหมาะสม".

examples.source: How to use the SPSA optimizer to estimate the ground-state energy
examples.translation: วิธีใช้ตัวปรับแต่ง SPSA เพื่อประมาณพลังงาน ground-state
examples.suggested: วิธีใช้ตัวหาค่าเหมาะที่สุด (optimizer) SPSA — หรือคง optimizer เป็นภาษาอังกฤษ
examples.why: ตัวปรับแต่ง leans toward 'tuner/adjuster' rather than 'optimizer'; defensible and used consistently (L22/41/43/158/165) so not misleading, but the textbook term is ตัวหาค่าเหมาะที่สุด.

examples.source: This makes it a natural candidate for quantum simulation.
examples.translation: ทำให้เป็นผู้สมัครที่เหมาะสมสำหรับการจำลองควอนตัม
examples.suggested: ทำให้เป็นตัวเลือกที่เหมาะสมสำหรับการจำลองควอนตัม
examples.why: ผู้สมัคร is a calque of English 'candidate' (carries an applicant/election sense); a native technical writer would use ตัวเลือก here.

editor_note: Reads as competent, well-edited Tagalog technical prose with the correct convention of keeping core domain terms (qubit, gate, circuit, channel, backend, Sampler, pulse, barrier, broadcast, metadata) in English; "tagubilin" for instruction and "timing" are used consistently, and the informal register holds throughout with no po/opo slips. Meaning is faithful end to end with no semantic drift, inversions, or broken examples. A few isolated naturalness slips (a literal calque of a pulse being "played," a clipped rendering of "operate on," and a redundant double "output") are translationese but do not mislead.

examples.source: It is an instruction played on all channels that are part of a communication procedure with the hub
examples.translation: Ito ay isang tagubilin na nilalaro sa lahat ng channel na bahagi ng isang proseso ng komunikasyon sa hub
examples.suggested: Ito ay isang tagubilin na ipinapatupad/ipinapadala sa lahat ng channel...
examples.why: 'nilalaro' literally means 'played' as in a game or music; for a pulse a native technical writer would use 'ipinapatupad' or 'ipinapadala'. Reads as a calque.

examples.source: Instruction - The gate and the qubit to operate on.
examples.translation: `Instruction` - Ang gate at ang qubit na dapat gawin.
examples.suggested: ...ang gate at ang qubit na pag-ooperasyunan (kung saang qubit ito gagamitin).
examples.why: 'ang qubit na dapat gawin' reads as 'the qubit that must be made/done' and loses the 'operate on' sense; slight flattening though understandable in context.

examples.source: The image of the circuit timing data output by draw_circuit_schedule_timing conveys the following information
examples.translation: Ang imahe ng output ng timing data ng circuit na na-output ng draw_circuit_schedule_timing ay naghahatid ng sumusunod na impormasyon
examples.why: Redundant double rendering of 'output' ('output ng... na na-output ng') is clumsy; one instance suffices. Meaning intact.

editor_note: The domain-term convention is sound and consistent (qubit, gate, circuit, function, string, query, null space, etc. are kept in English, matching field usage; informal register holds throughout with no po/opo). However the file fails on two meaning-level defects: "deduce s" is rendered as "ibukod ang s" (= exclude/set aside, the inverse of inferring s), inverting the punchline of the classical post-processing section; and "we conclude" leaves the English verb untranslated as "concludes tayo," ungrammatical raw-MT residue. With an additional "either way" mistranslated as "kahit saan" (everywhere), the prose mostly reads naturally but contains genuine drift a learner would be misled by.

examples.source: ...we can deduce s from the results of this computation.
examples.translation: ...maaari nating ibukod ang s mula sa mga resulta ng computation na ito.
examples.suggested: ...maaari nating mahinuha/matukoy ang s mula sa mga resulta...
examples.why: "ibukod" means to exclude/set aside — the opposite of deduce/infer. This inverts the key conclusion of the classical post-processing step.

examples.source: Using the formula from above we conclude that the probability...
examples.translation: Gamit ang formula mula sa itaas, concludes tayo na ang probabilidad...
examples.suggested: ...napagpasyahan/mahihinuha natin na ang probabilidad...
examples.why: The English verb "concludes" is left untranslated and inserted ungrammatically (subject "tayo" with an English 3rd-person verb) — raw machine-translation residue.

examples.source: Simon's algorithm and its analysis are basically the same either way.
examples.translation: ...ang algoritmo ni Simon at ang pagsusuri nito ay halos pareho kahit saan.
examples.suggested: ...halos pareho sa alinmang paraan.
examples.why: "either way" refers to the two cases (m=n vs m≠n), not a spatial "everywhere"; "kahit saan" flattens/changes the meaning.

examples.source: ...find s with very high probability...
examples.translation: ...para mahanap ang s na may napataas na probabilidad...
examples.suggested: ...na may napakataas na probabilidad...
examples.why: "napataas" = increased; the English asserts a *very high* probability. The claim is softened.

examples.source: ...will contain no other vectors in addition to those corresponding to 0^n and s...
examples.translation: ...hindi maglalaman ng ibang mga vector karagdagan sa mga katumbas ng 0^n at s...
examples.suggested: ...bukod pa sa mga katumbas ng 0^n at s...
examples.why: "karagdagan sa" is a stiff calque of "in addition to" — reads as translationese; a native writer would use "bukod pa sa".

examples.source: ...putting these strings into separate buckets... summing separately over all the buckets.
examples.translation: ...inilalagay...sa magkakahiwalay na mga timba...nang hiwalay sa lahat ng timba.
examples.suggested: keep "bucket" or use "grupo/lalagyan"
examples.why: "timba" is a literal water-pail; in a binning/grouping context it reads oddly. A native technical writer would keep "bucket" or use a grouping word.

editor_note: Reads as genuine, fluent Tagalog technical prose with confident, conventional handling of core domain terms kept in English (qubit, eigenstate, one-time pad, basis, fidelity, no-cloning theorem, entanglement) used consistently; informal register holds throughout with no po/opo slips. The main weakness is that "eavesdropper/eavesdropping" is rendered four different ways across the file (pag-eavesdrop, eavesdropper, mananawid, pakikinig/tagapakinig) — defensible individually but inconsistent. A few isolated naturalness/semantic slips ("Makakapagsalita ka ba" = "can you say" vs EN "can you think"; "Gaano katagal" implies duration not letter-length) and two typos exist, but none mislead a learner on the actual QKD physics or pedagogy.

examples.source: eavesdropping / eavesdropper / hunting the eavesdropper
examples.translation: pag-eavesdrop (L234,242) / eavesdropper (L295,524) / mananawid (L286, L4) / pakikinig, tagapakinig (L698,790,951,956)
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering of eavesdropping/eavesdropper and use it throughout (e.g. pag-eavesdrop + eavesdropper, or pakikinig + tagapakinig).
examples.why: Four competing renderings of the single most central adversary term in the module; each is defensible alone but the inconsistency hurts a teaching text that repeatedly contrasts the eavesdropping vs. no-eavesdropping cases.

examples.source: Can you think of others?
examples.translation: Makakapagsalita ka ba ng iba pa?
examples.suggested: Maiisip mo pa ba ang iba?
examples.why: "Makakapagsalita" means "can you say/speak," shifting the meaning from thinking-up other decryptions to speaking them; mild semantic drift, not misleading but inaccurate.

examples.source: But if many do not agree, something has gone wrong!
examples.translation: Ngunit kung marami ang hindi magkasundo, may nagkamaling!
examples.suggested: …may nagkamali! / may naganap na mali!
examples.why: "nagkamaling" leaves a dangling ligature with no following word — a native writer would write "may nagkamali" or "may mali"; reads as an unfinished phrase.

examples.source: How many letters long could a message be for it to be decrypted…
examples.translation: Gaano katagal ang mensahe para ma-decrypt…
examples.suggested: Gaano kahaba (ilang titik) ang mensahe na maaaring i-decrypt…
examples.why: "Gaano katagal" asks about temporal duration, whereas the English asks about character length; the surrounding answer makes intent recoverable, so it is a minor flatten rather than a hard error.

examples.source: the language here assumes that Eve actually makes a measurement
examples.translation: ang wika dito ay nagpapagpalagay na si Eve ay talagang gumagawa ng sukat
examples.suggested: …ay nagpapalagay na…
examples.why: "nagpapagpalagay" is a typo (duplicated "pag"); correct form is "nagpapalagay." Purely mechanical but visible in body prose.

editor_note: The prose itself is genuinely native and fluent — idiomatic Tagalog with the conventional code-switching a real TL technical writer uses (qubit/circuit/kernel/backend/Simulator Mode kept in English, Tagalog connective tissue like "Mga Gabay," "Pagpapatakbo ng code," "Magsimula dito"), consistent terminology, and correct informal register throughout. The FAIL is NOT about fluency but about semantic drift against the current English: the translation reflects an older source — it says "isa sa tatlong backend" and lists only 3 backends (dropping IBM Code Engine) where English says four and lists four, injects a "(1–2 min)" timing claim absent from the source, and carries a stray duplicated simulator-callout block inside the execution-backends details. These mislead, so they outweigh the otherwise strong prose.

examples.source: Click **Run** to execute them using one of four backends: 1. Binder ... 2. IBM Code Engine ... 3. Local Jupyter ... 4. Custom server
examples.translation: upang magpatakbo gamit ang isa sa tatlong backend: 1. Binder ... 2. Local Jupyter ... 3. Custom server
examples.why: Wrong quantity (four→tatlong/three) AND the entire 'IBM Code Engine' list item is dropped. The translation reflects an older 3-backend version of the source; readers lose a real execution option and get a count that contradicts the source.
examples.suggested: upang magpatakbo gamit ang isa sa apat na backend: ... 2. IBM Code Engine — Serverless Jupyter sa iyong IBM Cloud account. Cold start sa loob ng ilang segundo sa halip na minuto. ... (restore item 2 and renumber to four)

examples.source: The first click starts a Jupyter kernel via Binder or IBM Code Engine. After that, runs are instant.
examples.translation: Ang unang click ay magsisimula ng libreng Jupyter kernel sa pamamagitan ng Binder or IBM Code Engine (1–2 min). Pagkatapos noon, instant na ang mga pagpapatakbo.
examples.why: Inserts a quantitative claim '(1–2 min)' that the current English does not make (and adds 'libreng'/free not in this sentence). This is a stale caveat from an earlier source revision presented as current fact.
examples.suggested: Drop '(1–2 min)' and 'libreng': 'Ang unang click ay magsisimula ng Jupyter kernel sa pamamagitan ng Binder o IBM Code Engine. Pagkatapos noon, instant na ang mga pagpapatakbo.'

examples.source: (no equivalent — this block does not exist inside the 'Available execution backends' details in English)
examples.translation: Walang IBM Quantum account? I-enable ang Simulator Mode sa Settings para makapagpatakbo ng lahat ng code nang hindi nagsa-sign up.
examples.why: A duplicated simulator-callout block is injected inside the execution-backends details that has no counterpart in the source; it is leftover/stale content.
examples.suggested: Remove the stray block entirely so the details matches the English structure.

examples.source: Enter your API token and CRN in Settings once ... if you don't have one.
examples.translation: Ilagay ang inyong API token at CRN sa Settings nang isang beses lamang ... kung wala pa kayong account.
examples.why: Switches to formal/plural 'inyong/kayong' where the rest of the file uses informal singular 'iyong/mo/ka' (e.g. line 65 'iyong unang mga hakbang'). Minor register inconsistency, not misleading.
examples.suggested: Use informal singular for consistency: 'Ilagay ang iyong API token at CRN ... kung wala ka pang account.'

editor_note: Reads like native Tagalog technical prose: domain terms (qubit, gate, circuit, observable, expectation value, transpile, backend, SWAP gates, coupling map, gate cutting) are correctly kept in English per Filipino field convention and wrapped in natural Tagalog scaffolding ("isasaalang-alang natin", "kaya naman", "ayon sa pagkakabanggit"), used consistently throughout. Meaning is faithful end to end — parameter assignments, ideal outcomes (-1/+1), the 6^num_reps subexperiment count, and the cross-verify rationale all match the English exactly, and the pedagogical emphasis is preserved. Informal register holds with one isolated formal-plural slip ("kayong"); no semantic drift found.

examples.source: be sure you have the following installed
examples.translation: siguruhing mayroon kayong mga sumusunod na naka-install
examples.suggested: siguruhing mayroon kang mga sumusunod na naka-install
examples.why: Uses formal/plural 'kayong' here while the rest of the file uses casual singular natin/tayo; an isolated register slip, not misleading.

examples.source: The results could have been further improved
examples.translation: Ang mga resulta ay maaari pang mapabuti pa
examples.suggested: Ang mga resulta ay maaari pang mapabuti
examples.why: Redundant doubling of 'pa' (pang...pa); a native editor would drop one. Purely stylistic, meaning intact.

editor_note: Reads as native, well-edited Ukrainian technical prose — fluent imperative instructions with consistent informal "ти" and the conventional editorial "Рекомендуємо" for "we recommend." Domain/tooling terms are correct and idiomatic (редагований режим for editable mode, режим налагодження for debug mode). Meaning is faithful throughout with no drift, negation, or quantity errors; only the cosmetic notes below.

examples.source: Standard install / your global Python installation
examples.translation: Стандартна установка / глобальну інсталяцію Python
examples.suggested: optionally unify on інсталяція (or установка) for the install noun
examples.why: Minor within-file mixing of установка vs інсталяція for the install noun; both are accepted UK terms used in slightly different senses (action vs installed instance), so it does not mislead.

examples.source: Read the contributing guidelines
examples.translation: Прочитай керівництво з участі
examples.suggested: настанови щодо внеску
examples.why: 'керівництво з участі' is slightly literal (reads as 'guidelines on participation'); the link text 'зробити внесок' already carries the right contribution sense, so this is purely stylistic.

editor_note: Reads like genuine native Ukrainian technical prose, not MT: idiomatic phrasing and consistent, correct domain terminology (кубіт, вентиль, схема, синдром, перевертання біта/фази, теорема про заборону клонування, вентиль Адамара; CNOT kept in English). Informal "ти" holds and meaning is faithful with no semantic drift. Only a few isolated grammatical slips (preposition "зі"→"з" twice, one gender-concord error on "вимірювання") and a lesson/section term wobble keep it from a clean PASS — none mislead.

examples.source: From the mathematical expression for the state above
examples.translation: Зі математичного виразу для стану вище
examples.suggested: З математичного виразу для стану вище
examples.why: Wrong preposition variant: before "математичного" the correct form is "з", not "зі". Same slip recurs at line ~189 ("зі попереднього розділу"). Grammatical, not meaning-affecting.

examples.source: would be to perform a standard basis measurement
examples.translation: була б стандартна базисна вимірювання
examples.suggested: стандартне базисне вимірювання
examples.why: Gender-concord error: "вимірювання" is neuter, so adjectives must be neuter ("стандартне базисне"), not feminine. A native technical writer would not write this.

examples.source: which we'll see in the next lesson / in the last section of the lesson
examples.translation: в наступному розділі ... в останньому розділі уроку
examples.suggested: в наступному уроці
examples.why: "lesson" is rendered as "розділ" in line 14 but as "урок" elsewhere (lines 98, 141). Minor within-file inconsistency for the structural term; does not mislead but slightly blurs course navigation.

examples.source: the 3-bit repetition code is completely oblivious to phase-flip errors
examples.translation: 3-бітовий код повторення зовсім не реагує на помилки перевертання фази
examples.suggested: ... зовсім не виявляє помилок перевертання фази
examples.why: "не реагує" (does not react) is a slightly looser rendering of "oblivious/cannot detect"; meaning is preserved and idiomatic, so this is a stylistic note rather than a defect.

editor_note: The main body (intro through both VQE walkthroughs, ~lines 40-998) is genuinely excellent native technical Ukrainian — fluent, well-edited, consistent terminology (кубіт, гейт, спостережуваний, анзац, енергія основного стану, збіжність, заплутаність, суперпозиція), informal "ти" held throughout. But the closing third (Conclusion / Review / Appendix, lines 999-1267) collapses: the central term "анзац" silently switches to the non-standard "ансац/ансаци" 20+ times, "квантова схема" is replaced by the raw untranslated English "Circuit" ~8 times in running Ukrainian, and there are two real semantic errors — "barren plateaus" rendered as "барвисті плато" (colorful plateaus, the opposite meaning) and "variational quantum eigensolver" glossed as "власновектор" (eigenvector, the wrong object). This looks like the tail sections were spliced from a different, weaker pass; it fails on wrong domain terminology, ≥3 within-file term inconsistencies, and semantic drift, even though the bulk reads as a clear PASS.

examples.translation: ансац / ансаци
examples.suggested: анзац / анзаци
examples.why: The body correctly and consistently uses 'анзац' (~30+ times). The entire Conclusion/Review/Appendix switches to the non-standard 'ансац' 20+ times — a major within-file inconsistency on the single most important term of the module.

examples.source: parameterized quantum circuit / circuits
examples.translation: квантовий Circuit / Circuit-и / Circuit-у
examples.suggested: квантова схема / квантові схеми
examples.why: The English word 'Circuit' is left untranslated inside Ukrainian sentences in the closing sections, whereas the body correctly uses 'квантова схема' everywhere. Reads as raw, unfinished MT.

examples.source: regions where gradients vanish exponentially (barren plateaus)
examples.translation: барвисті плато
examples.suggested: безплідні плато (barren plateaus)
examples.why: 'barren' (desolate/empty) is mistranslated as 'барвисті' = colorful/vivid — the opposite connotation. A learner would be actively misled about this standard VQE failure mode.

examples.source: You've used VQE, a variational quantum eigensolver
examples.translation: варіаційний квантовий власновектор
examples.suggested: варіаційний квантовий розв'язувач власних значень
examples.why: The appositive gloss renders 'eigensolver' as 'власновектор' (eigenvector) — a different mathematical object. The title and rest of the file correctly use 'розв'язувач власних значень'.

examples.source: the resulting qubit Hamiltonian is simple
examples.translation: результівний кубітний Гамільтоніан
examples.suggested: отриманий / результуючий кубітний Гамільтоніан
examples.why: 'результівний' is a non-standard calque of 'resulting'; a native technical writer would use 'отриманий' or 'результуючий'. Minor but a clear translationese slip.

examples.source: after saving it to your environment / native gates of our backend
examples.translation: обліддних даних (typo); нативних гейтів нашого Backend
examples.suggested: облікових даних; бекенду / нашого backend (lowercase, consistent)
examples.why: Typo 'обліддних'→'облікових' (line 287); 'Backend' is left capitalized like a German noun and inconsistently cased versus body usage, reading awkwardly in Ukrainian prose.

editor_note: Fluent, native-sounding technical Ukrainian that genuinely teaches: idiomatic choices like "з чистого аркуша", "за лаштунками", and "важелів управління" show a human technical-writer hand, not MT, and meaning is faithful throughout with informal "ти" held consistently. The deliberate keep-English-terms convention (Qubit/Gate/Circuit/Backend/Transpiler) is applied consistently. Three small slips keep this from a clean PASS: a within-file coinage inconsistency ("тригубітний" vs the standard "однокубітний/двокубітний/n-кубітний" pattern), a mixed coherence term ("когеренція" in the intro vs "когерентність" later), and "хибний Backend" (line 107) which reads as "erroneous" rather than the intended mock/simulated "fake" — better matched by "фейковий Backend" used at lines 302/328.

examples.source: a 3-qubit Toffoli gate
examples.translation: тригубітний Gate Тоффолі
examples.suggested: трикубітний Gate Тоффолі
examples.why: The 'г' coinage is non-standard and breaks parallelism with 'однокубітні', 'двокубітний', and 'n-кубітного' used elsewhere in the same file. Use the consistent '-кубітний' stem.

examples.source: use a fake backend that represents the IBM Quantum Brisbane device
examples.translation: використаємо хибний Backend, що представляє пристрій IBM Quantum Brisbane
examples.suggested: використаємо фейковий Backend ...
examples.why: 'хибний' means false/erroneous, implying the backend produces wrong results; the FakeBrisbane/FakeVigo classes are mock/simulated backends. The translation itself uses 'фейковий Backend' at lines 302/328, so 'хибний' is both semantically weaker and inconsistent.

examples.source: finite coherence (T₁, T₂) impacts computations
examples.translation: скінченна когеренція (T₁, T₂) впливає на обчислення
examples.suggested: скінченна когерентність ...
examples.why: Coherence is rendered as 'когеренція' in the intro (lines 21, 27) but 'когерентність' in Section 3 onward (lines 298-302, 367-369). Both are defensible, but pick one for within-file consistency; 'когерентність' dominates.

examples.source: Fractional gates are parameterized quantum gates... / ...non-local gates... / ...fractional RX gates...
examples.translation: Дробові ворота - це параметризовані квантові ворота (L21) ... нелокальних гейтів (L363) ... дробові RX гейти (L445)
examples.suggested: Pick ONE rendering of "gate" and use it file-wide. Given the title is "дробових воріт", normalize the L363→end half from "гейт" back to "ворота" (нелокальних воріт, дробових воріт, RX ворота, U ворота, базисний вентиль/ворота).
examples.why: The single core domain term is split cleanly between the two halves of the file (ворота L4–299, гейт L363–593). This is a within-file inconsistency on the file's central concept that can mislead a learner into thinking two different objects are meant, and signals two unreconciled translation passes.

examples.source: ...transpile the circuit for a backend that does not support fractional gates.
examples.translation: ...транспілюємо схему для бекенду, який не підтримує дробові гейти.
examples.suggested: ...для backend, який не підтримує...
examples.why: "backend" is kept in Latin script everywhere else in the file (L116, L215, L228, L264, L299) but is transliterated as "бекенду" only here — a secondary consistency slip that tracks the same gate-term boundary.

examples.source: ...the specialized transpiler pass `FoldRzzAngle` is not invoked.
examples.translation: ...спеціалізований проход транспілера `FoldRzzAngle` не викликається.
examples.suggested: ...спеціалізований прохід транспілера...
examples.why: "проход" is a misspelling of "прохід" (pass); note the first-half rendering at L76/228 correctly uses "прохід". Minor orthographic slip isolated to the second, separately-translated half.
